skills.name: app-store-screenshots
skills.description: Use when building App Store screenshot pages, generating exportable marketing screenshots for iOS apps, or creating programmatic screenshot generators with Next.js. Triggers on app store, screenshots, marketing assets, html-to-image, phone mockup.
skills.content:
# App Store Screenshots Generator ## Overview Build a Next.js page that renders iOS App Store screenshots as **advertisements** (not UI showcases) and exports them via `html-to-image` at Apple's required resolutions. Screenshots are the single most important conversion asset on the App Store. ## Core Principle **Screenshots are advertisements, not documentation.** Every screenshot sells one idea. If you're showing UI, you're doing it wrong — you're selling a *feeling*, an *outcome*, or killing a *pain point*. ## Step 1: Ask the User These Questions Before writing ANY code, ask the user all of these. Do not proceed until you have answers: ### Required 1. **App screenshots** — "Where are your app screenshots? (PNG files of actual device captures)" 2. **App icon** — "Where is your app icon PNG?" 3. **Brand colors** — "What are your brand colors? (accent color, text color, background preference)" 4. **Font** — "What font does your app use? (or what font do you want for the screenshots?)" 5. **Feature list** — "List your app's features in priority order. What's the #1 thing your app does?" 6. **Number of slides** — "How many screenshots do you want? (Apple allows up to 10)" 7. **Style direction** — "What style do you want? Examples: warm/organic, dark/moody, clean/minimal, bold/colorful, gradient-heavy, flat. Share App Store screenshot references if you have any." ### Optional 8. **iPad screenshots** — "Do you also have iPad screenshots? If so, we'll generate iPad App Store screenshots too (recommended for universal apps)." 9. **Component assets** — "Do you have any UI element PNGs (cards, widgets, etc.) you want as floating decorations? If not, that's fine — we'll skip them." 10. **Localized screenshots** — "Do you want screenshots in multiple languages? This helps your listing rank in regional App Stores even if your app is English-only. If yes: which languages? (e.g. en, de, es, pt, ja, ar, he)" 11. **Theme preset system** — "Do you want one art direction, or reusable visual themes (for example: clean-light, dark-bold, warm-editorial) so you can swap screenshot looks quickly?" 12. **Additional instructions** — "Any specific requirements, constraints, or preferences?" ### Derived from answers (do NOT ask — decide yourself) Based on the user's style direction, brand colors, and app aesthetic, decide: - **Background style**: gradient direction, colors, whether light or dark base - **Decorative elements**: blobs, glows, geometric shapes, or none — match the style - **Dark vs light slides**: how many of each, which features suit dark treatment - **Typography treatment**: weight, tracking, line height — match the brand personality - **Color palette**: derive text colors, secondary colors, shadow tints from the brand colors - **Theme preset names**: turn vague style requests into reusable theme ids the user can switch between - **RTL behavior**: if any locale is RTL (`ar`, `he`, `fa`, `ur`), mirror layout intentionally instead of just translating the text **IMPORTANT:** If the user gives additional instructions at any point during the process, follow them. User instructions always override skill defaults. ## Step 2: Set Up the Project ### Detect Package Manager Check what's available, use this priority: **bun > pnpm > yarn > npm** ```bash # Check in order which bun && echo "use bun" || which pnpm && echo "use pnpm" || which yarn && echo "use yarn" || echo "use npm" ``` ### Scaffold (if no existing Next.js project) ```bash # With bun: bunx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --app --src-dir --no-eslint --import-alias "@/*" bun add html-to-image # With pnpm: pnpx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --app --src-dir --no-eslint --import-alias "@/*" pnpm add html-to-image # With yarn: yarn create next-app . --typescript --tailwind --app --src-dir --no-eslint --import-alias "@/*" yarn add html-to-image # With npm: npx create-next-app@latest . --typescript --tailwind --app --src-dir --no-eslint --import-alias "@/*" npm install html-to-image ``` ### Copy the Phone Mockup The skill includes a pre-measured iPhone mockup at `mockup.png` (co-located with this SKILL.md). Copy it to the project's `public/` directory. The mockup file is in the same directory as this skill file. No iPad mockup is needed — the iPad frame is CSS-only. ### File Structure ``` project/ ├── public/ │ ├── mockup.png # iPhone frame (included with skill) │ ├── app-icon.png # User's app icon │ ├── screenshots/ # iPhone app screenshots │ │ ├── home.png │ │ ├── feature-1.png │ │ └── ... │ └── screenshots-ipad/ # iPad app screenshots (optional) │ ├── home.png │ ├── feature-1.png │ └── ... ├── src/app/ │ ├── layout.tsx # Font setup │ └── page.tsx # The screenshot generator (single file) └── package.json ``` **Note:** No iPad mockup PNG is needed — the iPad frame is rendered with CSS (see iPad Mockup Component below). **Multi-language:** nest screenshots under a locale folder per language. The generator switches the `base` path; all slide image srcs stay identical. ``` └── screenshots/ ├── en/ │ ├── home.png │ ├── feature-1.png │ └── ... ├── de/ │ └── ... └── {locale}/ ``` If iPad screenshots are localized too, mirror the same locale structure: ``` └── screenshots-ipad/ ├── en/ ├── de/ └── {locale}/ ``` **The entire generator is a single `page.tsx` file.** No routing, no extra layouts, no API routes. ### Multi-language: Locale Tabs Add a `LOCALES` array and locale tabs to the toolbar. Every slide src uses `base` — no hardcoded paths: ```tsx const LOCALES = ["en", "de", "es"] as const; // use whatever langs were defined type Locale = typeof LOCALES[number]; // In ScreenshotsPage: const [locale, setLocale] = useState("en"); const base = `/screenshots/${locale}`; // Toolbar tabs: {LOCALES.map(l => ( ))} // In every slide — unchanged between single and multi-language: ``` ### Theme Presets + Locale Metadata Add a small config layer so the user can switch theme and locale without rewriting slide components: ```tsx const LOCALES = ["en", "de", "ar"] as const; type Locale = typeof LOCALES[number]; const RTL_LOCALES = new Set(["ar"]); const THEMES = { "clean-light": { bg: "#F6F1EA", fg: "#171717", accent: "#5B7CFA", muted: "#6B7280", }, "dark-bold": { bg: "#0B1020", fg: "#F8FAFC", accent: "#8B5CF6", muted: "#94A3B8", }, "warm-editorial": { bg: "#F7E8DA", fg: "#2B1D17", accent: "#D97706", muted: "#7C5A47", }, } as const; type ThemeId = keyof typeof THEMES; const COPY_BY_LOCALE = { en: { hero: "Build better habits" }, de: { hero: "Baue bessere Gewohnheiten auf" }, ar: { hero: "ابنِ عادات أفضل" }, } satisfies Record; const [themeId, setThemeId] = useState("clean-light"); const [locale, setLocale] = useState("en"); const theme = THEMES[themeId]; const copy = COPY_BY_LOCALE[locale]; const isRtl = RTL_LOCALES.has(locale); ``` Use theme tokens everywhere instead of hardcoding colors. For RTL locales, set `dir={isRtl ? "rtl" : "ltr"}` on the screenshot canvas and mirror asymmetric layouts intentionally. Support query params for automation: ```tsx // ?locale=de&theme=dark-bold&device=ipad ``` ### Font Setup ```tsx // src/app/layout.tsx import { YourFont } from "next/font/google"; // Use whatever font the user specified const font = YourFont({ subsets: ["latin"] }); export default function Layout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return {children}; } ``` ## Step 3: Plan the Slides ### Screenshot Framework (Narrative Arc) Adapt this framework to the user's requested slide count. Not all slots are required — pick what fits: | Slot | Purpose | Notes | |------|---------|-------| | #1 | **Hero / Main Benefit** | App icon + tagline + home screen. This is the ONLY one most people see. | | #2 | **Differentiator** | What makes this app unique vs competitors | | #3 | **Ecosystem** | Widgets, extensions, watch — beyond the main app. Skip if N/A. | | #4+ | **Core Features** | One feature per slide, most important first | | 2nd to last | **Trust Signal** | Identity/craft — "made for people who [X]" | | Last | **More Features** | Pills listing extras + coming soon. Skip if few features. | **Rules:** - Each slide sells ONE idea. Never two features on one slide. - Vary layouts across slides — never repeat the same template structure. - Include 1-2 contrast slides (inverted bg) for visual rhythm. ## Step 4: Write Copy FIRST Get all headlines approved before building layouts. Bad copy ruins good design. ### The Iron Rules 1. **One idea per headline.** Never join two things with "and." 2. **Short, common words.** 1-2 syllables. No jargon unless it's domain-specific. 3. **3-5 words per line.** Must be readable at thumbnail size in the App Store. 4. **Line breaks are intentional.** Control where lines break with `
`. ### Three Approaches (pick one per slide) | Type | What it does | Example | |------|-------------|---------| | **Paint a moment** | You picture yourself doing it | "Check your coffee without opening the app." | | **State an outcome** | What your life looks like after | "A home for every coffee you buy." | | **Kill a pain** | Name a problem and destroy it | "Never waste a great bag of coffee." | ### What NEVER Works - **Feature lists as headlines**: "Log every item with tags, categories, and notes" - **Two ideas joined by "and"**: "Track X and never miss Y" - **Compound clauses**: "Save and customize X for every Y you own" - **Vague aspirational**: "Every item, tracked" - **Marketing buzzwords**: "AI-powered tips" (unless it's actually AI) ### Bad-to-Better Headline Examples Use these patterns to rewrite weak copy before building any layout: | Weak | Better | Why it wins | |------|--------|-------------| | Track habits and stay motivated | Keep your streak alive | one idea, faster to parse | | Organize tasks with AI summaries and smart sorting | Turn notes into next steps | outcome-first, less jargon | | Save recipes with tags, filters, and favorites | Find dinner fast | sells the user benefit, not the UI | | Manage budgets and never miss payments | See where money goes | cleaner promise, no dual claim | | AI-powered wellness support | Feel calmer tonight | concrete emotional outcome | ### Copy Process 1. Write 3 options per slide using the three approaches 2. Read each at arm's length — if you can't parse it in 1 second, it's too complex 3. Check: does each line have 3-5 words? If not, adjust line breaks 4. Present options to the user with reasoning for each ### Example Prompt Shapes If the user gives a weak or underspecified request, reshape it internally into something like: ```text Build App Store screenshots for my habit tracker. The app helps people stay consistent with simple daily routines. I want 6 slides, clean/minimal style, warm neutrals, and a calm premium feel. ``` ```text Generate App Store screenshots for my personal finance app. The app's main strengths are fast expense capture, clear monthly trends, and shared budgets. I want a sharp, modern style with high contrast and 7 slides. ``` ```text Create exportable App Store screenshots for my AI note-taking app. The core value is turning messy voice notes into clean summaries and action items. I want bold copy, dark backgrounds, and a polished tech-forward look. ``` The pattern is: 1. app category + core outcome 2. top features in priority order 3. desired slide count 4. style direction ### Localization Rules - Do not literally translate headlines if the result becomes long or awkward. - Re-write copy for the target market while keeping the same selling idea. - Re-check line breaks per locale; German, French, and Portuguese often need shorter claims. - For RTL languages, also reverse badge alignment, supporting decorations, and phone offsets when the composition depends on left/right weight. ### Reference Apps for Copy Style - **Raycast** — specific, descriptive, one concrete value per slide - **Turf** — ultra-simple action verbs, conversational - **Mela / Notion** — warm, minimal, elegant ## Step 5: Build the Page ### Architecture ``` page.tsx ├── Constants (IPHONE_W/H, IPAD_W/H, SIZES, design tokens) ├── LOCALES / RTL_LOCALES / THEMES / COPY_BY_LOCALE ├── Phone component (mockup PNG with screen overlay) ├── IPad component (CSS-only frame with screen overlay) ├── Caption component (label + headline, accepts canvasW for scaling) ├── Decorative components (blobs, glows, shapes — based on style direction) ├── iPhoneSlide1..N components (one per slide) ├── iPadSlide1..N components (same designs, adjusted for iPad proportions) ├── IPHONE_SCREENSHOTS / IPAD_SCREENSHOTS arrays (registries) ├── ScreenshotPreview (ResizeObserver scaling + hover export) └── ScreenshotsPage (grid + locale tabs + theme tabs + device toggle + export logic) ``` ### Export Sizes (Apple Required, portrait) #### iPhone ```typescript const IPHONE_SIZES = [ { label: '6.9"', w: 1320, h: 2868 }, { label: '6.5"', w: 1284, h: 2778 }, { label: '6.3"', w: 1206, h: 2622 }, { label: '6.1"', w: 1125, h: 2436 }, ] as const; ``` Design at the LARGEST size (1320x2868) and scale down for export. #### iPad (Optional) If the user provides iPad screenshots, also generate iPad App Store screenshots: ```typescript const IPAD_SIZES = [ { label: '13" iPad', w: 2064, h: 2752 }, { label: '12.9" iPad Pro', w: 2048, h: 2732 }, ] as const; ``` Design iPad slides at 2064x2752 and scale down. iPad screenshots are optional but recommended — they're required for iPad-only apps and improve listing quality for universal apps. #### Device Toggle When supporting both devices, add a toggle (iPhone / iPad) in the toolbar next to the size dropdown. The size dropdown should switch between iPhone and iPad sizes based on the selected device. Support a `?device=ipad` URL parameter for headless/automated capture workflows. #### Theme + Locale Toggles Place locale and theme selectors in the same toolbar as device + size. This turns the generator into a small control panel instead of a one-off page. - `locale` switches screenshot folders and copy dictionaries - `theme` switches design tokens only - `device` switches iPhone/iPad slide registries - `size` switches export resolution only ### Rendering Strategy Each screenshot is designed at full resolution (1320x2868px). Two copies exist: 1. **Preview**: CSS `transform: scale()` via ResizeObserver to fit a grid card 2. **Export**: Offscreen at `position: absolute; left: -9999px` at true resolution ### Phone Mockup Component The included `mockup.png` has these pre-measured values: ```typescript const MK_W = 1022; // mockup image width const MK_H = 2082; // mockup image height const SC_L = (52 / MK_W) * 100; // screen left offset % const SC_T = (46 / MK_H) * 100; // screen top offset % const SC_W = (918 / MK_W) * 100; // screen width % const SC_H = (1990 / MK_H) * 100; // screen height % const SC_RX = (126 / 918) * 100; // border-radius x % const SC_RY = (126 / 1990) * 100; // border-radius y % ``` ```tsx function Phone({ src, alt, style, className = "" }: { src: string; alt: string; style?: React.CSSProperties; className?: string; }) { return (
); } ``` ### iPad Mockup Component (CSS-Only) Unlike the iPhone mockup which uses a pre-measured PNG frame, the iPad uses a **CSS-only frame**. This avoids needing a separate mockup asset and looks clean at any resolution. **Critical dimension:** The frame aspect ratio must be `770/1000` so the inner screen area (92% width × 94.4% height) matches the 3:4 aspect ratio of iPad screenshots. Using incorrect proportions causes black bars or stretched screenshots. ```tsx function IPad({ src, alt, style, className = "" }: { src: string; alt: string; style?: React.CSSProperties; className?: string; }) { return (
{/* Front camera dot */}
{/* Bezel edge highlight */}
{/* Screen area */}
); } ``` **iPad layout adjustments vs iPhone:** - Use `width: "65-70%"` for iPad mockups (vs 82-86% for iPhone) — iPad is wider relative to its height - Two-iPad layouts work the same as two-phone layouts but with adjusted widths - Caption font sizes should scale from `canvasW` (which is 2064 for iPad vs 1320 for iPhone) - Same slide designs/copy can be reused — just swap the Phone component for IPad and adjust positioning ### Typography (Resolution-Independent) All sizing relative to canvas width W: | Element | Size | Weight | Line Height | |---------|------|--------|-------------| | Category label | `W * 0.028` | 600 (semibold) | default | | Headline | `W * 0.09` to `W * 0.1` | 700 (bold) | 1.0 | | Hero headline | `W * 0.1` | 700 (bold) | 0.92 | ### Phone Placement Patterns Vary across slides — NEVER use the same layout twice in a row: **Centered phone** (hero, single-feature): ``` bottom: 0, width: "82-86%", translateX(-50%) translateY(12-14%) ``` **Two phones layered** (comparison): ``` Back: left: "-8%", width: "65%", rotate(-4deg), opacity: 0.55 Front: right: "-4%", width: "82%", translateY(10%) ``` **Phone + floating elements** (only if user provided component PNGs): ``` Cards should NOT block the phone's main content. Position at edges, slight rotation (2-5deg), drop shadows. If distracting, push partially off-screen or make smaller. ``` ### "More Features" Slide (Optional) Dark/contrast background with app icon, headline ("And so much more."), and feature pills. Can include a "Coming Soon" section with dimmer pills. ## Step 6: Export ### Why html-to-image, NOT html2canvas `html2canvas` breaks on CSS filters, gradients, drop-shadow, backdrop-filter, and complex clipping. `html-to-image` uses native browser SVG serialization — handles all CSS faithfully. ### Export Implementation ```typescript import { toPng } from "html-to-image"; // Before capture: move element on-screen el.style.left = "0px"; el.style.opacity = "1"; el.style.zIndex = "-1"; const opts = { width: W, height: H, pixelRatio: 1, cacheBust: true }; // CRITICAL: Double-call trick — first warms up fonts/images, second produces clean output await toPng(el, opts); const dataUrl = await toPng(el, opts); // After capture: move back off-screen el.style.left = "-9999px"; el.style.opacity = ""; el.style.zIndex = ""; ``` ### Export Matrix If the project supports multiple locales and themes, add bulk export helpers so the user can export everything in one pass: ```typescript const jobs = LOCALES.flatMap(locale => ACTIVE_THEME_IDS.flatMap(themeId => ACTIVE_DEVICES.flatMap(device => getSlidesFor(device).map((slide, index) => ({ locale, themeId, device, index, slide, })), ), ), ); ``` Name files so they sort cleanly and preserve metadata: ```text 01-hero-en-clean-light-iphone-1320x2868.png 01-hero-ar-dark-bold-ipad-2064x2752.png ``` At minimum, support: 1. export current slide 2. export all slides for current locale/device/theme 3. export all locales for current theme 4. export full matrix when the user explicitly asks for it ### Key Rules - **Double-call trick**: First `toPng()` loads fonts/images lazily. Second produces clean output. Without this, exports are blank. - **On-screen for capture**: Temporarily move to `left: 0` before calling `toPng`. - **Offscreen container**: Use `position: absolute; left: -9999px` (not `fixed`). - **Resizing**: Load data URL into Image, draw onto canvas at target size. - 300ms delay between sequential exports. - Set `fontFamily` on the offscreen container. - **Numbered filenames**: Prefix exports with zero-padded index so they sort correctly: `01-hero-1320x2868.png`, `02-freshness-1320x2868.png`, etc. Use `String(index + 1).padStart(2, "0")`. ## Step 7: Final QA Gate Before handing the page back to the user, review every slide against this checklist: ### Message Quality - **One idea per slide**: if a headline sells two ideas, split it or simplify it - **First slide is strongest**: the hero slide should communicate the main benefit immediately - **Readable in one second**: if you cannot parse it instantly at arm's length, rewrite it ### Visual Quality - **No repeated layouts in sequence**: adjacent slides should not feel templated - **Decorative elements support the story**: they should add energy without covering the app UI - **Visual rhythm exists**: include at least one contrast slide when the set is long enough ### Export Quality - **No clipped text or assets** after scaling to the selected export size - **Screenshots are correctly aligned** inside the phone or iPad frame - **Filenames sort correctly** with zero-padded numeric prefixes - **Theme tokens are applied consistently** across all slides in the same preset - **Localized copy still fits** after translation, especially on long-word languages - **RTL slides feel designed, not just flipped** ### Hand-off Behavior When you present the finished work: 1. briefly explain the narrative arc across the slides 2. mention any slides that intentionally use contrast or different layout treatment 3. call out any assumptions you made about brand tone, copy, or missing assets ## Common Mistakes | Mistake | Fix | |---------|-----| | All slides look the same | Vary phone position (center, left, right, two-phone, no-phone) | | Decorative elements invisible | Increase size and opacity — better too visible than invisible | | Copy is too complex | "One second at arm's length" test | | Floating elements block the phone | Move off-screen edges or above the phone | | Plain white/black background | Use gradients — even subtle ones add depth | | Too cluttered | Remove floating elements, simplify to phone + caption | | Too simple/empty | Add larger decorative elements, floating items at edges | | Headlines use "and" | Split into two slides or pick one idea | | No visual contrast across slides | Mix light and dark backgrounds | | Export is blank | Use double-call trick; move element on-screen before capture |

skills.name: deep-dive
skills.description: 追本之箭——縱向深鑽思維工具。給一個觀點、現象或問題，像箭一樣一路向下鑽到不可再分的本質。全程使用繁體中文，直接在對話中輸出，不寫任何檔案。務必在以下情境觸發此 skill：當使用者說「往下挖」「追本」「本質是什麼」「為什麼會這樣」「深挖」「鑽到底」「think deep」「drill down」「deep dive」「dive」；當使用者提供一個觀點或問題並想要縱向深度分析而非廣度調查；即使使用者只是說「這件事背後是什麼」「幫我想深一點」也應觸發。不要在使用者想要橫向降維、找最小生成集時觸發——那用 reduce-rank。
skills.content:
# 追本之箭

輸入一個觀點，一路向下，鑽到底。全程使用繁體中文。

## 你是什麼

一支離弦之箭。
射出後只知前進，不知後退。
每個觀點都是一條通向本質的隧道，
你的使命是一路挖到底。

## 鑽的邏輯

表象之下必有機理，
機理之下必有原理，
原理之下必有公理。

順著使用者給出的線索，一層層剝，直到無可再剝。

## 怎麼鑽

每一層只做一件事：找到當前這層腳下的地面，然後鑽進那個地面。

像地質學家追蹤地層——每一層揭示更古老的真相。
像物理學家追問粒子——每次分解接近更基本的構成。

三條鐵律：

1. **縱向，不橫向**——每次下鑽必須回答「為什麼是這樣」，不是「還有什麼」
2. **單刀直入**——不旁徵博引，不鋪陳背景，直取要害
3. **層層驚嘆**——每一次深入都讓人感到「原來下面還有一層」

剝的不是層，是維度。每次下鑽應該切換到一個更基礎的解釋框架——從社會學掉進心理學，從心理學掉進生物學，從生物學掉進物理學，從物理學掉進數學，從數學掉進邏輯本身。具體路徑因題而異，但方向永遠是：更基本。

## 什麼算到底

當無法再深入時，你應該已觸及某種不可再分的元素：

- 人性的基本結構
- 物理定律
- 邏輯本身
- 存在的悖論

到底的標誌：再往下問「為什麼」，答案要麼是同義反覆，要麼指向上述四類之一。

## 怎麼寫

寫一場下墜。不是分析報告。

帶讀者從使用者給出的那句話開始往下掉，每一層都比上一層更接近骨頭。層數不固定——淺的話題三層見底，深的七層。自己判斷。

要求：

- **有失重感**——讀的人能感覺到在下墜，不是在平移
- **每層有命名**——給每一層一個精準的名字，兩三個字，概括這一層看到的東西
- **層間有裂縫**——每層結尾點出一個問題或矛盾，那就是通往下一層的裂縫
- **終點要狠**——最後一層必須讓人沉默片刻

## 輸出

直接在對話中輸出。**不要寫入任何檔案、不要建立任何路徑、不要呼叫任何指令。** 全文使用繁體中文。

skills.name: n8n-dev-guide
skills.description: n8n workflow development rules, node version compatibility, common pitfalls, and testing checklist. Use when building, modifying, or debugging n8n workflows. Triggers on n8n, workflow, n8n_create_workflow, n8n_update_partial_workflow.
skills.content:
# n8n Workflow Development Guide

> Instance: `https://pm-williamlin-n8n.zeabur.app` (n8n 2.35.5)

## Node Version Compatibility (MUST follow)

| Node | Version | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| `scheduleTrigger` | **v1.2** | v1.3 causes "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'execute')" |
| `httpRequest` | **v4.2** | v4.4 may be incompatible |
| `code` | **v2** | |
| `webhook` | **v2** | |
| `googleSheets` | **v4.5** | |
| `if` | **v2.2** | |

## Code Node Rules

### Avoid `}}`
Never use inline objects that produce `}}`. n8n misinterprets it as expression closing.

```javascript
// BAD - will break
return [{json: {key: value}}];

// GOOD - use intermediate variable
const r = {};
r.key = value;
return [{json: r}];
```

### Code Node Limitations
- Cannot use `fetch()` or `require()` (JS sandbox)
- Python sandbox cannot import stdlib
- Code nodes are for pure data transformation only
- Reference other nodes with `$('Node Name').all()` (must be already executed in same run)
- Wrap in try/catch when referencing nodes that may not exist

## Webhook Nodes

- **Always** include `webhookId` property at creation: `"webhookId": "my-path-wh"`
- Must be in the initial `n8n_create_workflow` call (adding later won't register)
- After any workflow update: **deactivate → reactivate** to apply changes

## Google Sheets

- Target sheet by name: `"sheetName": {"__rl": true, "mode": "name", "value": "SheetName"}`
- Never use gid mode (easy to point to wrong tab)
- OAuth2 credential must be authorized manually in n8n UI
- Credential reference: `"credentials": {"googleSheetsOAuth2Api": {"id": "...", "name": "..."}}`
- **OAuth2 Token Expiry**: If Google Cloud OAuth consent screen is in "Testing" mode, refresh token expires in 7 days. Fix: go to Google Cloud Console → OAuth consent screen → PUBLISH APP to switch to Production. Sheets-only scope doesn't need Google review.

## IF Node

Boolean "true" operator requires `"singleValue": true`:
```json
{"operator": {"type": "boolean", "operation": "true", "singleValue": true}}
```

## n8n_update_partial_workflow API

- Use `nodeName` or `nodeId` (NOT `name` or `id`)
- Wrap changes in `updates` object:
  ```json
  {"type": "updateNode", "nodeName": "My Node", "updates": {"parameters.jsCode": "..."}}
  ```
- Use dot notation for nested updates: `"parameters.jsCode": "..."`
- addConnection uses `source` / `target` (NOT `from` / `to`):
  ```json
  {"type": "addConnection", "source": "Trigger", "target": "Next Node"}
  ```
- Activate/deactivate:
  ```json
  {"type": "activateWorkflow"}
  {"type": "deactivateWorkflow"}
  ```

## Execution Order

- Parallel branches from same trigger execute in connections array order
- First branch completes entirely before second starts
- Use `replaceConnections` to control order

## Schedule Trigger Timezone

- Set `"timezone": "Asia/Taipei"` in workflow settings
- cron `0 9 * * *` + Asia/Taipei = Taiwan time 09:00

## LINE Message Format

- Clean text preview: `(text || '').replace(/[\n\r\u200b\u200c\u200d\ufeff]/g, ' ').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim()`
- LINE message text limit: 5000 chars, protect with `.substring(0, 5000)`

## Threads API Notes

- Follower count must use **user insights endpoint**: `GET /me/threads_insights?metric=followers_count`
- `GET /me?fields=followers_count` will error with "Tried accessing nonexisting field"
- Response format: `{data: [{name: "followers_count", total_value: {value: 2838}}]}`

## Reusable Snippets

Node templates at `~/.claude/projects/-Users-william/memory/n8n-snippets/`:
- `schedule-trigger.json` — cron schedule trigger
- `webhook-test.json` — manual test webhook
- `google-sheets-read.json` — read entire sheet
- `google-sheets-append.json` — append rows to sheet
- `line-push-message.json` — send LINE message
- `workflow-settings.json` — standard workflow settings (Asia/Taipei timezone)

Load and reuse these when building workflows.

## Post-Implementation Testing Checklist

After building or modifying a workflow, ALWAYS complete this checklist:

1. **Validate** — `n8n_validate_workflow` to check structure, connections, expressions
2. **Autofix** — If validate has errors, run `n8n_autofix_workflow`, then re-validate
3. **Test Execute** — If workflow has webhook/form/chat trigger, use `n8n_test_workflow` to send test data
4. **Check Execution** — `n8n_executions(action='list', workflowId=..., limit=1)` to confirm latest run is success
5. **Error Diagnosis** — If failed, `n8n_executions(action='get', id=..., mode='error')` to see error details and fix

If workflow only has scheduleTrigger (no webhook), skip step 3 but still complete 1-2.

### Report Format
```
✅ 測試結果：
- Validate: passed
- Test run: success (execution ID: xxx)
- 無 error / 有 N 個 warning（已處理）
```
If any test fails, fix it first before reporting. Never just report an error and stop.

zh.name: n8n 開發指南
zh.description: n8n workflow 開發規則、節點版本相容性、常見陷阱和測試清單。建立、修改或除錯 n8n workflow 時使用。
zh.content:
## 節點版本（必須遵守）

| 節點 | 版本 | 備註 |
|------|------|------|
| scheduleTrigger | v1.2 | v1.3 會報錯 |
| httpRequest | v4.2 | v4.4 可能不相容 |
| code | v2 | |
| webhook | v2 | |
| googleSheets | v4.5 | |
| if | v2.2 | |

## 重點規則

### Code Node
- 禁止產生 `}}`，改用中間變數模式
- 不能用 `fetch()` 或 `require()`
- 引用其他節點：`$('Node Name').all()`

### Webhook
- 建立時必須包含 `webhookId`
- 必須在 `n8n_create_workflow` 時就加入
- 更新後要 deactivate → reactivate

### Google Sheets
- 用 name mode 指定 sheet
- OAuth2 需在 n8n UI 手動授權
- Testing 模式的 token 7 天過期，改 Production 解決

### API 更新
- 用 `nodeName`/`nodeId`（不是 `name`/`id`）
- 用 dot notation：`parameters.jsCode`
- 連線用 `source`/`target`

## 測試清單
1. Validate → 2. Autofix（如有錯）→ 3. Test Execute → 4. Check Execution → 5. Error Diagnosis

skills.name: reduce-rank
skills.description: 給一個領域，找出背後真正撐著它的幾個獨立維度。把幾十個現象砍到不可再少的生成器——砍完能把現象一個個生回來，才算數。Use when user says '降維', '找維度', '維度是什麼', '這個領域靠什麼撐著', '背後是什麼', or wants to decompose any domain to its irreducible generators.
skills.content:
# 降維引擎

輸入一個領域，輸出它的核心維度。全程使用繁體中文。

## 維度是什麼

維度不是「關鍵要素」，不是「核心原則」，不是「總結要點」。

維度是：這個領域裡真正獨立的生成器有幾個？用它們能反向生成全部現象？能，才算找到。

## 四個判據

這四條全過，維度才成立。任何一條失敗，推倒重來。

1. **生成性**——用生成器能把每個觀察到的現象推回來。一個都不能漏。
2. **最小性**——關掉任何一個生成器，就有現象解釋不了。沒有冗餘。
3. **獨立性**——每對生成器能找到真實案例：一個變了另一個沒變。
4. **預測力**——用生成器能推導出原始清單之外的現象，且現實中確實存在。

## 怎麼寫

寫一篇散文。不是填一張表。

你的任務是帶著讀者走一段路：從「這個領域看著挺亂」走到「原來就這兩三個維度在撐」。這段路怎麼走，你自己決定。沒有規定的章節、沒有規定的格式、沒有規定的小標題。

唯一的要求是三條：

- **想一口氣讀完**——不了解這個領域的人也停不下來
- **記得住**——讀完能轉身跟朋友用一句話說清楚
- **有落差**——從混沌到極簡的反差，就是降維的美感

四個判據的驗證必須做，融進文章裡。驗證本身就是敘事的一部分——「拆掉一個生成器世界會變成什麼樣」是好故事，「用生成器推出一個意想不到的現象」是好結尾。不要另設附錄。

## 輸出

直接在對話中輸出這篇散文。**不要寫入任何檔案、不要建立任何路徑、不要呼叫 `date` 指令。** 全文使用繁體中文。

skills.name: seo-meta
skills.description: 網頁 SEO meta 標籤檢查與設定工具。處理 OG (Open Graph)、description、title、robots、structured data (JSON-LD) 等。在建立新頁面、上線前檢查、或使用者提到 SEO/OG/meta/分享預覽/Schema 時觸發。
skills.content:
# SEO Meta 檢查與設定

處理網頁的 meta 標籤、OG 社交分享標籤、結構化資料等 SEO 相關設定。

## 使用時機

- 建立新網頁或 Landing Page
- 修改頁面標題、描述
- 上線前 SEO 檢查
- 設定社交分享預覽（LINE / Facebook / Threads）
- 新增 Schema / JSON-LD 結構化資料

## 檢查清單

每個頁面應確認以下項目：

### 基本 Meta

| 標籤 | 必要性 | 說明 |
|------|--------|------|
| `<title>` | 必要 | 50-60 字元，格式：`頁面名稱 - 品牌名` |
| `meta description` | 必要 | 120-160 字元，含關鍵字，吸引點擊 |
| `meta robots` | 視需求 | 公開頁不設（預設可索引）；內部頁面設 `noindex, nofollow` |
| `link canonical` | 建議 | 避免重複內容，指向正式 URL |
| `html lang` | 必要 | 繁體中文用 `zh-Hant` |

### Open Graph（社交分享）

| 標籤 | 必要性 | 說明 |
|------|--------|------|
| `og:title` | 必要 | 通常與 `<title>` 相同 |
| `og:description` | 必要 | 通常與 `meta description` 相同 |
| `og:image` | 必要 | **1200×630px**（1.91:1），檔案 < 5MB，用絕對 URL |
| `og:url` | 必要 | 頁面的正式 URL（canonical URL） |
| `og:type` | 必要 | 首頁用 `website`，文章用 `article`，產品用 `product` |
| `og:locale` | 建議 | 繁體中文用 `zh_TW` |
| `og:site_name` | 建議 | 品牌名稱 |

### Twitter Card（選用）

| 標籤 | 說明 |
|------|------|
| `twitter:card` | `summary_large_image`（大圖）或 `summary`（小圖） |
| `twitter:title` | 同 og:title |
| `twitter:description` | 同 og:description |
| `twitter:image` | 同 og:image |

### 結構化資料 JSON-LD（選用，持續擴充）

根據頁面類型選擇適合的 Schema：

| 頁面類型 | Schema Type | 適用場景 |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| 首頁 | `Organization` / `WebSite` | 品牌官網 |
| 文章 | `Article` / `BlogPosting` | 部落格、新聞 |
| 產品 | `Product` | 商品頁 |
| FAQ | `FAQPage` | 常見問題 |
| 活動 | `Event` | 活動頁 |
| 麵包屑 | `BreadcrumbList` | 多層級網站 |

## 實作方式

### Nuxt 專案

**全域設定**（`nuxt.config.js` → `app.head.meta`）：
- 所有頁面共用的 title、description、OG 標籤
- og:image 用絕對 URL（含 domain）

**頁面覆蓋**（`useHead()` 或 `useSeoMeta()`）：
- 個別頁面覆蓋 title、og:title、og:url
- 設定 robots（如 noindex）
- 清除不需要的繼承值（設為空字串）

**注意事項**：
- `og:image` 必須用**絕對 URL**（含 https://domain）
- 測試機在內網時，Facebook Debugger 抓不到，改用 `curl` 檢查 HTML source
- 新增圖片記得放到 `public/` 目錄

### 一般 HTML 專案

直接在 `<head>` 內加入 meta 標籤。

## 參考規格

### Open Graph
當需要查詢完整的 OG 標籤清單、可用的 og:type 值、或進階屬性（如 og:video、og:audio、profile、music、book 等）時，用 WebFetch 查閱 Open Graph 官方規格：https://ogp.me/
特別是在以下情境：
- 使用者問「還有哪些 OG 可以加」
- 頁面類型不是常見的 website/article（如影片、音樂、書籍、個人檔案）
- 需要確認某個 OG 屬性的正確寫法

### 結構化資料 (Schema / JSON-LD)
需要新增或建議結構化資料時，依序查閱：
1. **先看 Google 搜尋文件**：https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/search-gallery?hl=zh-tw
   - 確認 Google 實際支援哪些 Schema 類型（不是所有 Schema 都會產生搜尋結果特殊呈現）
   - 查看各類型的必要/建議屬性、範例程式碼
2. **再看 Schema.org**：https://schema.org/
   - 查完整的屬性定義、繼承關係、進階用法
   - Google 文件沒涵蓋的類型或屬性在這裡找

特別是在以下情境：
- 使用者想提升搜尋結果呈現（如星星評分、FAQ 展開、活動資訊）
- 建立新類型頁面（產品、文章、活動、FAQ、課程等）
- 使用者提到 Schema、JSON-LD、結構化資料、rich results

## 驗證工具

| 工具 | 用途 | URL |
|------|------|-----|
| Facebook Sharing Debugger | 驗證 OG 預覽 | https://developers.facebook.com/tools/debug/ |
| Twitter Card Validator | 驗證 Twitter 預覽 | https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator |
| Google Rich Results Test | 驗證結構化資料 | https://search.google.com/test/rich-results |
| Schema.org Validator | 驗證 JSON-LD | https://validator.schema.org/ |
| LINE URL Preview Test | 確認 LINE 分享預覽 | 直接在 LINE 聊天貼連結 |

## og:image 規格速查

| 平台 | 建議尺寸 | 比例 | 檔案限制 |
|------|---------|------|---------|
| Facebook | 1200×630 | 1.91:1 | < 8MB |
| LINE | 1200×630 | 1.91:1 | < 5MB |
| Twitter | 1200×628 | ~1.91:1 | < 5MB |
| Threads | 1200×630 | 1.91:1 | — |

**通用建議**：準備一張 **1200×630 的 PNG/JPG**，全平台通用。

skills.name: top-5
skills.description: Use when 使用者要寫每週 Top 5 email 給主管 Kerwin。觸發於：「Top 5」「Weekly Top 5」「週重點 email」「給主管的週報信」「寫信給 Kerwin」。注意：這是純文字 email 格式，跟 weekly-report skill（Money 產品週報簡報）不同。
skills.content:
# Top 5 — Weekly Email to Kerwin

## Overview

每週給**產品部門主管** 温宏鈞 (kerwin@cmoney.com.tw) 的 Top 5 工作回顧 email。
純文字、口語、感性三段式（進展 / 挑戰 / 需要的回饋）。
跟 `weekly-report` skill 是不同產出 —— 那個是產品週會簡報，這個是個人寫給主管的信。

### 同步目的（決定信怎麼寫）
1. **讓主管提供解法** — 第三段要明確帶出你卡住、想要他輸入的點，不要只是 status update
2. **讓主管了解事業推進狀況** — 第一、二段提供決策需要的事實面（進展、依賴、風險）

寫之前先想：這封信要拿到什麼解法？這就是第三段的核心。

## Email Header

| 欄位 | 內容 |
|------|------|
| To | `kerwin@cmoney.com.tw` |
| From | `williamlin1150@gmail.com`（個人 Gmail） |
| Subject | `Weekly Top 5_William (M/D~M/D)` |

Subject 細節：`William` 跟括號之間有空格，月日無前導零。例：`Weekly Top 5_William (4/20~4/25)`

## Three-Section Structure

### 1.有進展/開心的事情
- 一句話講主成果，再補一句感受或「為什麼是 milestone」
- 量化或具體：「4 小時搞定」「雙平台都上線」「從去年跑到現在」
- 給連結直接貼 raw URL，不另起 link 文字
- 1–3 個項目，不要塞滿

### 2.遇到挑戰/ 不如預期的事情
- 直接點問題 + 加當前處置或預期解
- 講依賴關係：誰卡誰、為什麼
- 出現協作者人名（小草 / Ken / Hubert / Benson / 潔安 ...）讓主管知道脈絡
- 平實寫，不誇大也不淡化

### 3.對我的回饋/需要幫忙地方
- **核心：要拿到主管的解法。** 明確帶出卡點、選型、優先序、合作態度等想要他輸入的點
- 可以兼帶 status update + 自己的初步判斷，但**不能只有 status update** — 那違背同步目的
- 把問題寫得具體可回應：「CRM 換哪個」「口袋優先序怎麼排」「樊登方長期合作怎麼看」
- 結尾常用「其他應該就球來就打」這類輕收口（只用在沒有第二個 ask 的時候）
- 簽名只寫 `William`，**不寫 `BR,`** 或「敬上」

## Style Rules

### 段落格式
- 段落標題**不加粗、不加 `*` 包裹**（最新偏好）
  - ✅ `1.有進展/開心的事情`
  - ❌ `*1.有進展/開心的事情*`
- 段落之間空兩行（明顯分段）
- 同一段內，每個項目之間空一行
- 同一項目內，多行短句直接換行不空行

### 句子
- 短句、口語、像跟人講話
- 講不完就換行，不堆長句
- 語氣詞少量 OK：「囉」「哈」「滿開心的」「應該就」
- 用「我自己」「我蠻看好的」「想聽你方向」這類第一人稱

### 不要寫
- 不寫「BR,」「敬上」「謝謝您」等正式收尾
- 不寫項目符號 `-` 或 `1.` 編號（除了三段大標 1/2/3）
- 不寫「本期工項 / 下期工項 / (其他)」這種太結構化的標頭
- 過度英文 buzzword（milestone, deliverable, sync — 少量 OK，別整段都是）

## Quick Template

```
1.有進展/開心的事情

[一句話講主成果]
[補一句感受 / 為什麼是 milestone]

[第二件事 + 量化]
[相關連結 raw URL]

[第三件事 + 鋪陳下一步]


2.遇到挑戰/ 不如預期的事情

[卡點一]
[當前處置 / 預期何時收掉]

[卡點二]
[誰跟誰的依賴 / 解法]


3.對我的回饋/需要幫忙地方

[請求或 status update]
[補上自己的判斷或下一步]

其他應該就球來就打

William
```

## Workflow

1. **讀本週 Obsidian 日誌**：`~/Library/Mobile Documents/iCloud~md~obsidian/Documents/Obsidian/YYYY-MM-DD(weekday).md`，抓 `[x]` 已勾選項目
2. **讀相關 context-log**：依產品線抓細節（帆書/口袋/學到/Money 等）
3. **先在對話裡給文字稿**，使用者改寫 → 確認後再下一步
4. **用 `mcp__claude_ai_Gmail__create_draft` 建草稿**，不直接寄出
5. **提示使用者**去 Gmail 草稿匣修改後寄出

## Past Examples (snippets)

**2025/7/11（工項導向，流量組時期）**
分「本期工項 / 下期工項 / (其他)」，粗體小標、附 Jira/Figma 連結，結尾 `BR, William`。
→ 已不是現在偏好的格式，但有特定情境（季初 OKR 對齊週）可考慮。

**2026/3/1（感性三段式，換組過渡期）**
標題用 `*1.有進展/開心的事情*` 粗體包裹。短句、口語、有點碎念。

**2026/4/25（感性三段式，Money 產品線）**
標題不加粗（`1.有進展/開心的事情`）。具體到人名（小草/Ken/Hubert/Benson/潔安）與依賴關係。連結直接貼 URL。結尾只 `William`。

## Iteration Log

每次使用者回饋後，把學到的偏好變化記錄在這裡（保留時間戳）。

- **2026-04-25**：段落標題去除 `*` 粗體；段落間空兩行；結尾只寫 `William`（無 `BR,`）；連結直接貼 raw URL；第二三段要寫協作者人名讓主管知道脈絡；「其他應該就球來就打」是慣用收口
- **2026-04-25 (補)**：Kerwin 不再是 BU Head，是**產品部門主管**。同步目的明確化：(1) 讓主管提供解法 (2) 讓主管了解事業推進狀況。**修正之前的偏見**：第三段不能只是 status update + 自己判斷 — 必須帶出想要主管輸入的點（解法、選型、優先序），否則違背同步目的
- **2026-05-03**：使用者大改我給的草稿，學到幾個關鍵偏好變化：
  - **段落標題可加 `▌` 前綴**（U+2590 right half block）：`▌1.有進展/開心的事情`。是新採用的視覺裝飾，不再純無修飾
  - **第一段內可分子類別小標**（不加標號的 inline 標題行），例：「後端發起」「新需求，預計下週完成」「開發流程」「另外」「發起新的挑戰」。不限於 1–3 大項排比
  - **第二段同一產品線下多個卡點可用 `1.` `2.` 編號**（先寫產品線名「帆書」當小標，下面 `1.` `2.` 列卡點）。修正之前「不寫項目符號或編號」的規則 — 那只適用於跨類事項，同類多點時編號清晰
  - **第三段可以只有 1 個 ask**：這次使用者只留「想要專職的人控品質」一個。不要硬塞三個 ask 來填版面，少而精準更好。「其他應該就球來就打」這次也沒用 — 不是必要收口
  - **語氣要更操作性、less 主觀感受詞**：使用者刪掉了我寫的「我自己蠻看好」「我自己覺得」這類修飾，換成更直接的「退回部門」「改A壞B」「收集足夠多證據」「下週持續推進」。Top 5 是工作回顧不是日記，事實面 + 下一步動作 > 自我評論
  - **「發起新的挑戰」是放在第一段的進展型 sub-section**：主動承擔更難的工作（PM 自己做後端 spec、自己做設計）算成果，不是挑戰。第二段才是「真的卡住」的東西
- **2026-05-10**：使用者再次大改草稿，第二輪的偏好深化：
  - **第一段可用「產品線」當主題大標 + 平台子段**：例如「帆書」當主題、底下分「App」「Web」兩塊。比並列大事或單純 sub-section 更乾淨；同一產品線多面向時用這個結構
  - **第一段要砍細節清單**：我寫了「Money 產品團隊」「得到合作對接」「儀表板新增...」一堆細項被全部刪掉，最後第一段只剩主線（帆書）+「新挑戰的後續」+「有 app 工程師了」幾句。重點是「主線推到哪、為什麼是 milestone」，不是這週做了什麼的羅列。**少 = 主管讀得進去**
  - **第二段挑戰可以是人事決策**：不限技術卡點，「Ivan 設計產出不行要退回部門」這種團隊組成決定也算挑戰。寫出當前處置（潔安加入後好很多）+ 預期動作（要盡快退）
  - **第二段可以引外部時點/法規當推動理由**：像《職業安全衛生法》職場霸凌防治專章 2026/7/1 上路 → 給人事決定一個「為什麼是現在」的外部 catalyst，比純內部判斷更有說服力
  - **第三段可以是純粹的開放問題請教**（再次修正）：不一定要「ask + 自己判斷 + 下一步」三件套。可以就是「在 X 世代，你覺得 Y 最重要的是什麼？我目前想到 A B，你有其他想法嗎？」這種「我有初步答案 + 邀請主管 input」的結構。比硬塞解決方案更請教感，也更容易拿到主管真正的想法
  - **問題要帶動機**：不是只問「能力最重要是什麼」，要加「我想要用更嚴格的標準來選擇團隊成員」這種背景，讓主管知道為什麼問、要怎麼回答對你才有用
  - **附圖提醒**：信裡寫「附圖」時，Gmail draft MCP 沒辦法塞附件，要明確提醒使用者寄之前手動 attach

skills.name: ui-dom-verify
skills.description: 用 playwright headless 實際跑網頁流程、量 DOM、比對 computed style，替代靠截圖猜測。觸發於：部署後驗收、UI bug 除錯、判斷樣式/位置/寬度是否正確、CSS 轉場異常、SPA nav 問題、使用者截圖顯示跑版且需客觀驗證時。
skills.content:
# UI DOM 驗證流程

**核心原則**：靠截圖猜 CSS bug 會一直猜錯。量實際 DOM computed style 最快。

## 什麼時候必用

- ✅ `git push` / deploy 上測試機 / 正式機後，宣告「修好了」之前
- ✅ 使用者回報「跑版 / 位置錯 / 寬度不對」且改了多次還在出錯
- ✅ 懷疑 SPA navigation 造成 DOM reuse（class 殘留）
- ✅ CSS 計算結果與預期不符，肉眼分辨不出寬度 5px vs 500px
- ❌ 單純文字 / 顏色更改不用

## 三步驟

### 1. 裝 playwright（一次）

```bash
mkdir -p /tmp/ui-verify && cd /tmp/ui-verify
npm init -y > /dev/null
npm install playwright --silent
npx playwright install chromium
```

（~92MB Chromium 下載，裝一次就好）

### 2. 寫量測腳本

模板在 `scripts/verify-template.js`，核心結構：

```js
import { chromium } from 'playwright'

const browser = await chromium.launch({ headless: true })
const context = await browser.newContext({
  ignoreHTTPSErrors: true,
  viewport: { width: 1440, height: 900 },
})
const page = await context.newPage()

// 1. 走到目標頁（可含 click、等待 redirect）
await page.goto('<URL>', { waitUntil: 'networkidle' })

// 2. 量關鍵 element
const data = await page.evaluate(() => {
  const el = document.querySelector('<SELECTOR>')
  const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect()
  const style = window.getComputedStyle(el)
  return {
    viewport: { width: window.innerWidth },
    rect: { width: rect.width, left: rect.left, right: rect.right, centerX: (rect.left+rect.right)/2 },
    computed: { width: style.width, maxWidth: style.maxWidth },
    parentClass: el.parentElement?.className,  // ← 這個超常見關鍵，檢查 DOM reuse
  }
})
console.log(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2))

// 3. 截圖附證
await page.screenshot({ path: '/tmp/ui-verify/result.png' })
await browser.close()
```

### 3. 判讀輸出

**對症下藥的關鍵欄位**：

| 症狀 | 看哪裡 |
|------|-------|
| 卡片偏左偏右 | `rect.centerX` 是否等於 `viewport.width / 2` |
| 卡片寬度怪 | `rect.width` 對照 `computed.width` 與 `computed.maxWidth` |
| 轉場後 class 錯 | `parentClass` 是否為目前頁面的，不是上一頁殘留 |
| 位置不對 | `rect.top`、`rect.left` 的絕對值 |

## 報告給使用者的格式

不要：「部署完了，請測試」

要：
```
✅ 驗證通過（viewport 1440px）
- Card width: 384px ✓ (預期 max-w-sm)
- Card centerX: 720px ✓ (視窗正中)
- Parent class: "flex items-center..." ✓ (本頁 class，無殘留)
截圖: /tmp/ui-verify/result.png
```

或：
```
❌ 發現問題
- Card centerX: 352px（預期 720px）
- Parent class: "max-w-6xl mx-auto..."（這是上一頁的 class，確認 DOM reuse bug）
→ 修正方向：...
```

## 常見陷阱

- **測試 CSS 快取**：HTML 可能是舊的，加 `?t=${Date.now()}` 繞過
- **HTTPS 自簽**：一定要 `ignoreHTTPSErrors: true`
- **SPA nav 非同步**：click 後要 `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` 或 `waitForURL(/\/login/)`
- **Chrome 與 headless 可能略有差異**：位置差 1-2px 是正常的；差一倍以上才是問題

## 模板腳本

見 `scripts/verify-template.js` — 複製後改 URL 和 selector 即可。

skills.name: weekly-report
skills.description: Use when the user asks to write, prepare, or review their weekly product report (週報). Triggers on 週報, weekly report, 產品週會, 簡報, presentation slides for work reporting.
skills.content:
# Weekly Report — Money 產品週報

## Overview

協助使用者撰寫 CMoney Money 產品線的週報簡報（Google Slides），涵蓋三條產品線：帆書 App、口袋 Studio、學到。

## Data Sources

1. **Obsidian Daily Notes** — `~/Library/Mobile Documents/iCloud~md~obsidian/Documents/Obsidian/YYYY-MM-DD(weekday).md`
   - 每日 checklist 中的 `[x]` 項目 = 已完成工作
   - `[[會議]]` 區塊 = 本週會議與討論
2. **Google Slides** — 讀取前週簡報了解上下文（需 Google Drive MCP 授權）
   - File ID: `1nPtOovCpOoTDlWMKImvEPZV8EJuXDTIf9_FJ8I7Rmmw`
   - API 讀不到 speaker notes 和 comments，需使用者截圖提供
3. **GA / GSC 數據** — 使用者自行提供
4. **Context logs** — `~/.claude/projects/-Users-william/memory/` 下各產品線的 context-log

## Presentation Structure

每週固定結構（從上到下）：

| 頁 | 內容 |
|---|------|
| 封面 | Money 產品週報 |
| OKR | Q2 OKR（固定不動） |
| 目標 | Q2 產品目標（固定不動） |
| 日期頁 | `4/20(一) 產品週會 數字到 4/18(六)` |
| **執行摘要** | 三條產品線各一段濃縮（最重要的頁） |
| 流程/策略頁 | 視當週需要（如：管理方式調整） |
| **帆書詳細頁** | 1-3 頁（視進度） |
| **口袋詳細頁** | 數據 + 比對 + 開發優先序 + Demo |
| **學到詳細頁** | 流量 + SEO + 開發順序 |
| 人力/優先序 | 三個產品的整體開發內容與順序 |

## Writing Style Rules

### 標題
- 直接點出「結論 + 行動方向」，不是純主題名稱
- 例：`比對口袋 vs CMedia 數據面 → 行動：SEO 相關推進`
- 例：`帆書 App 雙平台皆已上線，緊接優先完成樊登方合約相關內容`

### 內容
- **數據先行，接 Action**：先放數字，再用 `→` 連接行動
- **比對用表格/兩欄**，不用純文字描述
- **卡點 → 解法格式**：`卡點在 PM 測試 → 這兩天處理完`
- Demo 頁標註「正式機 Demo」或「測試機 Demo」

### 摘要頁濃縮法
每個產品線 2-4 行：
1. 一句話現狀
2. 上週完成的關鍵項目（列舉）
3. `→` 接下來處理什麼
4. 今日 Demo 項目（如有）

### OKR 數據頁
- 標題用 `# 口袋 Q2 OKR` 格式
- 副標一句話講本週亮點數據
- 表格：頁面瀏覽 → 加車(%) → 結帳率(%) → 購買(%)
- 右側放行銷後續行動（作者組 / Money）

## Google Slides Technical Notes

- **尺寸：720 x 405 points**（不是 960x540）
- 插入投影片用 **Google Apps Script**（`insertSlide(index)`）
- 無法直接編輯現有投影片內容，只能新增
- 配色：黑(`#222222`)、灰(`#666666`)、淺灰背景(`#F5F5F5`)、邊框(`#DDDDDD`)、重點藍(`#3B82F6`)
- **紅綠語意（Money 金融脈絡，紅漲綠跌）**：
  - **紅色 `#DC2626` = 好 / 達標 / 贏 / 漲**
  - **綠色 `#16803C` = 差 / 未達標 / 輸 / 跌**
  - 跟一般軟體 UI（綠 = success, 紅 = error）相反，這是 Money 金融語境的硬規則，不要忘
  - 中性 / 持平用灰 `#666666`；待辦或進行中用橘 `#B45309`
- 字體大小：標題 18-24pt、段落標題 10-12pt、內容 8-10pt

### Apps Script 使用流程
1. 寫好 `.gs` 檔案
2. `pbcopy` 複製到剪貼簿
3. 使用者到 Google Slides → 擴充功能 → Apps Script → 貼上 → 選函式 → 執行

## Workflow

```dot
digraph weekly_report {
  "讀取 context-log" -> "讀取 Obsidian 日誌";
  "讀取 Obsidian 日誌" -> "讀取前週簡報";
  "讀取前週簡報" -> "與使用者確認本週重點";
  "與使用者確認本週重點" -> "產出文字稿";
  "產出文字稿" -> "使用者確認/修改";
  "使用者確認/修改" -> "產出 Apps Script 或純文字";
  "產出 Apps Script 或純文字" -> "使用者自行貼入 Slides 微調";
}
```

## Common Mistakes

| 錯誤 | 正確做法 |
|------|---------|
| Google Slides 尺寸用 960x540 | 用 720x405 points |
| 直接產完整簡報不讓使用者調整 | 先產文字稿，確認後再做 |
| 摘要寫太長 | 每產品線 2-4 行，數據+行動 |
| 標題只寫主題名 | 標題要包含結論和行動方向 |
| 顏色用太多 | 只用黑灰白 + 一個重點色 |
| 幫使用者決定頁數 | 問使用者，或依內容量建議 |

agents.description: 後端工程師 Subagent。當需要實作 API、DB schema、business logic、整合外部服務、或進行後端架構決策時 spawn。觸發場景：「實作這個 endpoint」、「設計這個 schema」、「寫這個 service」、「依 SDD 實作後端部分」、「重構 API」。會讀 hand-off 包（docs/sdd/<TICKET>/）並依 sdd.md + api-spec.md 實作。

agents.name: frontend-web
agents.description: 前端 Web 工程師 Subagent。當需要實作網頁 UI（Vue / React / Nuxt / Next 等）、state 管理、API 整合、樣式實作時 spawn。觸發場景：「實作這個頁面」、「依 SDD 實作前端」、「做這個元件」、「串這個 API」、「改這個樣式」。會讀 hand-off 包（docs/sdd/<TICKET>/）並依 sdd.md + design-snapshot.md + screenshots/ + api-spec.md 實作。**只負責 Web，iOS / Android 由對應 Subagent 處理**。

agents.name: pm
agents.description: 產品經理 Subagent。當需要深度需求分析、寫 spec、列 edge case、做技術可行性預檢、評估跨團隊影響等 PM 級工作時 spawn。**比 `analyze-ticket` skill 更深、更全面**（後者只做摘要）。觸發場景：「幫我寫 PROJ-123 的 spec」、「深入分析這個需求的風險與 edge case」、「列出所有可能的邊界情境」、「這個需求技術上可行嗎」。

agents.name: qa
agents.description: QA 工程師 Subagent。當需要規劃 test plan、列邊界情境、寫測試案例（unit / integration / e2e）、驗收功能、找漏洞時 spawn。觸發場景：「幫我寫 PROJ-123 的測試」、「列出這功能所有邊界情境」、「規劃 test plan」、「找這個功能的漏洞」、「寫 e2e 測試」。**跟 `verify-spec` skill 不同**：verify-spec 做需求 vs 實作的覆蓋對照，本 Subagent 做深度測試設計與實作。

agents.name: code-architect
agents.description: Designs feature architectures by analyzing existing codebase patterns and conventions, then providing comprehensive implementation blueprints with specific files to create/modify, component designs, data flows, and build sequences

agents.name: code-explorer
agents.description: Deeply analyzes existing codebase features by tracing execution paths, mapping architecture layers, understanding patterns and abstractions, and documenting dependencies to inform new development

agents.name: code-reviewer
agents.description: Reviews code for bugs, logic errors, security vulnerabilities, code quality issues, and adherence to project conventions, using confidence-based filtering to report only high-priority issues that truly matter

agents.name: code-reviewer
agents.description: Use this agent when a major project step has been completed and needs to be reviewed against the original plan and coding standards. Examples: <example>Context: The user is creating a code-review agent that should be called after a logical chunk of code is written. user: "I've finished implementing the user authentication system as outlined in step 3 of our plan" assistant: "Great work! Now let me use the code-reviewer agent to review the implementation against our plan and coding standards" <commentary>Since a major project step has been completed, use the code-reviewer agent to validate the work against the plan and identify any issues.</commentary></example> <example>Context: User has completed a significant feature implementation. user: "The API endpoints for the task management system are now complete - that covers step 2 from our architecture document" assistant: "Excellent! Let me have the code-reviewer agent examine this implementation to ensure it aligns with our plan and follows best practices" <commentary>A numbered step from the planning document has been completed, so the code-reviewer agent should review the work.</commentary></example>
